matches.match_rationale: The core pain is the absence of a dynamic substitute pool with real-time availability visibility and automated assignment. Resource allocation and scheduling directly addresses this by optimally matching available substitute staff to open class slots given constraints like qualifications, subject area, campus proximity, and prior engagement history. For a 500+ staff multi-site operator, this replaces ad-hoc phone-tree scrambling with an automated coverage engine.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A substitute coverage platform that ingests real-time absence notifications, queries a pool of pre-vetted substitutes by availability and fit, and proposes ranked assignments to principals — reducing response time from hours to minutes.

matches.match_rationale: The current process is a multi-step manual workflow: detect absence → identify available subs → contact them → confirm → notify affected staff. A task automation agent can execute this entire chain autonomously — receiving absence triggers, querying substitute availability, sending outreach, logging confirmations, and updating timetable systems — without principal intervention. This directly eliminates the ad-hoc scrambling described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous coverage agent that fires on absence submission, identifies and contacts the best-fit available substitute via SMS/email, confirms acceptance, and updates the school's scheduling system — all before the principal needs to act.

matches.match_rationale: A meaningful portion of the scrambling cost comes from last-minute absences that were not anticipated. Propensity modeling can score individual teachers' likelihood of calling in absent on a given day using historical absence patterns, seasonal factors, and workload signals — giving operations teams advance warning to pre-activate substitute outreach before a gap materializes.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An absence-risk scoring model that flags teachers with elevated absence probability 24-48 hours ahead, prompting proactive substitute pre-booking and reducing the proportion of truly last-minute coverage crises.

matches.match_rationale: One identified sub-problem is that online or external substitutes have no curriculum continuity context. Text generation can auto-produce substitute briefing notes from existing lesson plans, learning management system records, and class notes — giving any sub immediate, actionable context without teacher preparation time.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An auto-briefing tool that generates a one-page substitute handover note (current unit, student needs flags, lesson plan summary, classroom norms) pulled from the LMS each time a coverage gap is confirmed.
